reviews.review_msg: Michele is extremely sweet and gives great feedback. Her lectures get a little dry sometimes, but she is always trying to make jokes around class. There are assignments every week for the final portfolio, so as long as you're on top of that, you will do fine.

Michele was actually a really great prof! She's caring, her office hours and very helpful and make sure to bring your assignments to her to read over. However, she is a tough grader, she doesn't give out anything above a B+ easily, so you must work hard in this course to get the grade you want.

Read your theory from the text and read your feedback on your assignments.  Gives you a great framework of theory to apply to your assignments.  Michele is also very postitive, dynamic, has personality galore and cloaks her intellectual abilities with a nuturing teaching style.

You don't take this class for an easy A. You really have to work for it, BUT it's totally worth it. You will learn things that will really help you in your undergrad career. She's also very understanding about deadlines and such. Very approachable.
Very nice and approachable professor,  but she is an extremely hard marker. Make use of office hours as she will proof read and edit your papers for you!

It's a hard class, specially if you're taking it straight out of highschool. Also in the english department they very rarely give out A+ so its a toughie. Prof was very helpful and liked to engage students - the seminar format is really great - and make sure you go to office hours and make any adjustmentscorrections that she suggests.

Overall she is a decent prof who tries to engage students and is very into her teaching as well. However, she is a tough marker and don't always provide help even when it is promised (my half-hour appointment was made into 5 and no extra office hour was provided). Show up to class and read and follow the textbook. Long lectures.

She is an extremely hard marker, don't expect an A in her class, if you can scrap by with a B, consider yourself lucky. You forfeit your participation grade if you skip class twice. Giltrow worshipper, she has an unhealthy obsession with her, my biggest mistake was taking this class in my first year do not take it unless ABSOLUTELY necessary.

The TM is in charge of the online class. This class is quite hard I managed to get a B. But this class is no walk in the park. It's basically all assignments (writing) and 1 midterm. The marking for essays is like 4th year marking. So pretty difficult.

I never liked high school writing. I reluctantly took this course because my second degree required it. There was a lot of readings but they were useful. I liked this course because its explanations made sense. My strong vocabulary helps me in writing "abstractions" -- may be a student should take this in his final year? Or may be 1st year? Hmm...

Michele is the type of prof that you either get or you don't, she is very eccentric and can seem a bit odd at times, but the foundation of what she is teaching is really good. She is more flexible with upper level classes and will modify the readings and assignments based on our feedback (there was 10 of us in a class)

She isn't very organized but tries to be helpful by answering any questions. She loves the textbook's author so you have to write according to the textbook guidelines to get good marks. This course has some potential to improve your writing but I do not recommend taking this class with her.

Extremelly hard marker, way too many assignments, very vague and unhelpful. My friend who missed a class was not able to receive ANY of the missing handouts and the prof wasn't able to make extra time for her to give her extra help. Very disorganized and doesnt reply to important emails. Class is way too long (3 hrs) and could be condensed to 1 hr

hard marker! She is disorganized, always said she would email things, then "forgot". she ended up giving 5 assignments 2 weeks before end of term to complete, though she was supposed to assign one per week. I oncereturned to find everyone gone, and my laptop was left behind.  She couldn't wait 2 mins for me to return. Class is always out early,

The course has useful material and she is good at teaching and getting the class involved. Only thing is that she is a tough marker! She is very approachable and will help you a lot, but that doesn't necessarily mean you'll get an A. Pretty much have to live and breath english to do well.

She is easy to approach and always tries to help you with your work or any questions you have. Her marking tends to be unfair, so don't expect an easy A. Workload isn't light, but it's doable. Giltrow's books is used for this class, so never forget to emphasize and apply ideas from Giltrow's book into your papers. Class is always dismissed early.

this class offers very valuable information that will help you in university to write research papers. However that said, Michele is very unclear, unorganized, extremely unfair and she never gives back positive feedback. for a first year course DO NOT expect an easy mark.

I took this course online so the prof is irrelevant.  It depends on your TA; they mark everything.  I thought this was a very easy english course, I'm not particularly strong in it.  You can get a good mark by just using the techniques taught in your essay (force them all in).  It also really improved my overall writing skills as well.

I really liked her, I actually took the course with her a few semesters ago and I still think of her. I still use some of the stuff I learned in that class, especially reading papers and writing summaries. Always keep her notes around even though I'm doing health sciences.

She changed my perception on writing entirely. I learnt the importance of understanding the rationale of the writing styles behind each medium and how to integrate them to gain the trust of the reader. This was why she didn't give specific guidelines. She wanted us to develop the sensitivity to adjust our writing style in accordance to the context.

This class is not an easy one but it's extremely helpful if you need to learn how to write research articles. I loved the prof, you could go see her during office hours and she'll help you with the assignment before it's due so it pretty much guarantees a good grade. No final, and she tells you exactly what you'll be writing about on the midterm.

Funny/nice. Helpful too, b/c she will take a look at your assignments before they are due if you ask. Way too many assignments, especially towards the end of the course! If you are already with a heavy course load and are struggling to juggle assignments this course will break your back. Teaches more about concepts, features of academic writing.

She's pretty easygoing and friendly; let us off a little early which is a real plus since it was a 3 hour seminar in the afternoon. Unlike the other 199 profs, her style is more- I don't know- writing "theory" or "concept". Don't expect to walk in there and be told what to do to get an A on your essays. As a result though, course isn't that hard.

Worst prof I ever had at SFU, enough said. I tried going to her office hour for help due to my poor grades, she replied back to me saying "it's not my job to deal with everyday matters" She's basically telling me my bad grades in this course is an everyday thing... I've never been this mistreated by a prof before in my life. Don't take her!!

Very enthusiastic, disorganized at times. This class has a lot of work but should be manageable if you keep up and don't procrastinate. If you go to her office hours she is a lot of help for your assignments.

Go to class, ask questions, and go to her office hours for help (she looks over everything, which is really useful). The class is hard, but it'll help you with your writing. Our class didn't have an exam which was good, and as long as you kept up with assignments, it wasn't too hard! Just make sure to get your work checked by her first.
She is a really nice and responsible teacher. I really learnt a lot from her class. The questions are recommended during the class. If you wanna take English 199w, take her class!

Giltrow worshiper! Erratic person.She could be funny & happy and then just lose it.She went off on a student in class-stopping their presentation to argue with them because she had a differing opionion,creating an INCREDIBLY awkward environment to be in.She was so flaky & disorganized,and her attitude made it incredibly hard to want to go to class.
Waste of my time.  Worship Giltrow and be fancy with a highlighter during her make work projects its easy sailing.
Most useful class I have ever and will ever take. It paid off with an A+ on my first paper in another class this semester. Michele is VERY friendly, helpful and approachable :)
She is so enthusiastic and adorable. The class was super easy and there was relatively little work despite a few major projects. Participation helps too.

Michelle is a fun teacher and is very helpful.  She does a good job of answering the questions that we have in class, and always emails out a TO Do list for every week.  The main problem with the class is that there is so much homework, and it's 3 hours long.  But I can see how it will help me in the future.
At times disorganized but always very enthusiastic and eager to help
Totally disorganized & flakey.  I dreaded going to this class each week.  Just a slog through pointless make-work assignments.

Very nice lady who is pretty helpful. Makes class seem like highschool. Way too much homework given out. Portfolio hand ins do get confusing as to what goes in, and what stays out. Work is easy, but there is TONS of it.

The first time I took this class was through Distance Ed and I failed it because I totally didn't understand it. Then I took it (in-class)again, this time with Michele and she made it so much easier to understand. The weirdest thing she said was that she wanted to join the circus. Other than that, she's really nice and very helpful!

She is somewhat eccentric and a little hard to undestand. My group misinterpreted her instructions for an assignment, but ended up getting a better grade and doing a play out of it! Also, go for the Harbour Centre class, its more laidback than her main campus classes (thats what she says) and to top it off, we went to a bar for our last class!!!

Such a sweetheart! The course itself it really useful, but id recommend taking it w/ Michele! She's fun and crazy. Class reminded more like highschool. A nice break from the big lectures and hard finals.

The class in and of itself is very helpful. Now Michele...yes borderline krazy, eccentric, odd, but she made the class so memorable. Late, disorganized, unclear sometimes, but very very funnie, odd/interesting sense of humour, lenient, very nice, friendly, and you're either laughing WITH her or AT h
This course would have been so boring if it weren't for the energy (bordering on insane)of a caffinated Michele each week.  Take this course with her...
No Comments
ALWAYS LATE. easy marker. kinda flakey but easy grade in a hard class is always a plus
Very unorganized.  Very flexible with deadlines.

very very unclear on what she wants for portfolio.  but you should just put everything u have in there to show you improved over time.  Also learn a lot from her actually.  though u need to try very hard to follow wut she says.
Horribly unorganized, portfolio assignments unclear, sends emails out last minute (i.e. 6 hours before lecture).
Definately unclear marking scheme.  Not very organized either, but she's nice.
seems crazy, but has good ideas. open to different ideas and is personable
Really odd women, boring class, bordering on crazy.  Unclear marking scheme.
WONDERFUL!! interesting, pleasant, Enjoyable
No Comments
Inspiring, enthusiastic, very knowledgeable and light-hearted.
